steps.title: Identify the angle relationship at the intersection
steps.content:
Transversal $t$ intersects line $q$, creating angles on either side of the intersection point.

The angle labeled $x°$ is on the left side of the transversal, and the angle labeled $142°$ is on the right side—both positioned along the straight line $q$.

These two angles form a linear pair.

steps.title: Apply the linear pair property
steps.content:
Angles that form a linear pair are adjacent angles along a straight line, so they are supplementary and sum to $180°$:

$$x + 142 = 180$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
